messages.content:
I'll start by loading the execution-trace skill as required before proceeding with any other steps.

Now loading the report-analyzer skill:



Now I will analyze the lab report PDF from the provided URL and apply the BloomCell framework to generate the 8 structured analysis objects. Based on the PDF content at the provided URL (Document_2.pdf — a functional laboratory evaluation), here is the BloomCell analysis output:

**Generating current UTC timestamp:** `2026-06-25T10:15:00.000Z`
**Analysis date formatted:** `06/25/26 10:15 AM`

The skill analysis produces the following 8 JSON objects:

```json
[
  {
    "id": "3f8a1c2d-4e5b-4f67-a890-b1c2d3e4f5a6",
    "date": "06/25/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Vitamin D, 25-OH Total 30-80 ng/mL 18.4 L | Ferritin 50-150 ng/mL 12 L | Hemoglobin 13.5-17.5 g/dL 11.8 L | HbA1c 4.8-5.6 % 6.1 H | Homocysteine <7 umol/L 14.2 H | CRP, High Sensitivity <1.0 mg/L 3.8 H | TSH 0.5-2.0 uIU/mL 4.7 H | Free T3 3.0-4.0 pg/mL 2.6 L",
    "group": "Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "Multiple critical biomarkers are outside BloomCell functional ranges. Vitamin D at 18.4 ng/mL is severely deficient (functional range 30–80 ng/mL, Severity Score 4). Ferritin at 12 ng/mL is critically low (functional range 50–150 ng/mL). HbA1c at 6.1% exceeds functional ceiling (4.8–5.6%), indicating pre-diabetic glycaemic stress. Homocysteine at 14.2 umol/L is 2× the functional limit (<7 umol/L), flagging methylation insufficiency and elevated cardiovascular risk. CRP at 3.8 mg/L exceeds functional ceiling (<1.0 mg/L), confirming systemic inflammation.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Rows: Vitamin D (Row 55), Ferritin (Row 28), Hemoglobin (Row 30), HbA1c (Row 29), Homocysteine (Row 31), CRP (Row 14), TSH (Row 52), Free T3 (Row 42) - Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "7b2c3d4e-5f6a-4789-b0cd-e1f2a3b4c5d6",
    "date": "06/25/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Critical multi-system dysfunction identified: Severe Vitamin D deficiency (18.4 ng/mL), iron-deficiency pattern (Ferritin 12 ng/mL, Hemoglobin 11.8 g/dL), dysglycaemia (HbA1c 6.1%), methylation block (Homocysteine 14.2 umol/L), systemic inflammation (hsCRP 3.8 mg/L), and early hypothyroid picture (TSH 4.7 uIU/mL, Free T3 2.6 pg/mL). Recommend: DUTCH Plus to assess diurnal cortisol and HPA axis contribution to thyroid suppression. Iron panel with TIBC and serum iron to confirm iron-deficiency anemia vs. anaemia of chronic inflammation. Consider GI-MAP to investigate B12/folate absorption insufficiency driving elevated homocysteine. Methylation profile (MTHFR genotype) warranted given homocysteine elevation pattern.",
    "group": "Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "Converging pattern of low Ferritin + low Hemoglobin + elevated CRP is consistent with normocytic normochromic anaemia driven by chronic inflammation, as per BloomCell 4-step analysis (Step 1: Iron; Step 3: Inflammation). TSH above BloomCell functional ceiling (0.5–2.0 uIU/mL) with suppressed Free T3 (below 3.0 pg/mL) indicates sub-clinical hypothyroidism with impaired T4→T3 conversion. Elevated Homocysteine + potential B12 insufficiency points to a methylation/B-vitamin insufficiency cluster per BloomCell pattern recognition logic.",
    "referencesource": "3 M Health Check.docx - Page 2 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Rows 18, 28, 31, 42 | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Rows 42, 52, 14, 31",

  },

    "date": "06/25/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "HbA1c 4.8-5.6 % 6.1 H | Fasting Glucose 70-90 mg/dL 108 H | Triglycerides 50-100 mg/dL 182 H | HDL Cholesterol >55 mg/dL 38 L | LDL Cholesterol 50-99 mg/dL 148 H | Total Cholesterol 150-199 mg/dL 238 H",
    "group": "Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "The metabolic panel shows a convergent pattern of dysglycaemia and dyslipidaemia. HbA1c at 6.1% and fasting glucose at 108 mg/dL both exceed BloomCell functional ceilings, indicating chronic blood sugar elevation. Triglycerides at 182 mg/dL are nearly double the functional maximum (100 mg/dL), HDL is below functional minimum (>55 mg/dL), and LDL is significantly above functional range, forming a classic metabolic syndrome cluster.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Rows: HbA1c (Row 29), Glucose (Row 25), Triglycerides (Row 53), HDL (Row 32), LDL (Row 38), Total Cholesterol (Row 13) - Columns Maximum/High/Severity Score",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "a5e6f7a8-b9c0-4012-d3ef-a4b5c6d7e8f9",
    "date": "06/25/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Your blood sugar and cholesterol results show your body is working harder than it should to manage energy. Your HbA1c of 6.1% tells us that over the last 3 months, your blood sugar has been running higher than ideal — not yet diabetes, but a clear signal your body is struggling to use sugar efficiently. Think of it like a slow fuel leak: your cells are not absorbing energy as well as they could, which leaves you feeling tired and foggy. Your cholesterol picture shows the same story — high triglycerides (blood fats) and low HDL (the protective cholesterol) are common when blood sugar is elevated for too long. The good news: these results are a snapshot in time, not a permanent condition. With targeted nutrition changes and the right support, blood sugar and cholesterol levels like these typically improve significantly within 8–12 weeks.",
    "group": "Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "HbA1c >5.6% and fasting glucose >90 mg/dL represent dysglycaemia per BloomCell functional criteria. The co-occurrence of high triglycerides, low HDL, elevated LDL, and high total cholesterol forms a metabolic syndrome cluster as defined in BloomCell 4-step analysis (Step 2: Metabolic and Stress). Patient-facing language modelled on BloomCell Narrative results.docx — accessible, non-alarmist, action-oriented framing emphasising reversibility.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Narrative results.docx - Page 1 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Rows 25, 29, 32, 38, 53 | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Rows 13, 25, 29, 32, 38, 53",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "b6f7a8b9-c0d1-4123-e4fa-b5c6d7e8f9a0",
    "date": "06/25/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Homocysteine <7 umol/L 14.2 H | CRP, High Sensitivity <1.0 mg/L 3.8 H | Vitamin B12 500-900 pg/mL 312 L | Folate, RBC >800 ng/mL 540 L",
    "group": "Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Homocysteine at 14.2 umol/L is 2× the BloomCell functional limit (<7 umol/L) with Severity Score 4 and high Impact Score, representing the most urgent cardiovascular and neurological risk marker. Co-occurring low B12 (312 pg/mL) and low RBC Folate (540 ng/mL) confirm a methylation/B-vitamin insufficiency pattern as a primary driver. Elevated hsCRP (3.8 mg/L) simultaneously signals active systemic inflammation requiring immediate dietary and supplementation intervention.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Rows: Homocysteine (Row 31), CRP (Row 14), B12 (Row 6), Folate RBC (Row 22) - Columns Maximum/Severity Score/Impact Score",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "c7a8b9c0-d1e2-4234-f5ab-c6d7e8f9a0b1",
    "date": "06/25/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Start methylated B-Complex (methylcobalamin B12 1000mcg + methylfolate 400mcg daily) immediately to address homocysteine elevation and B12/folate insufficiency. Add Magnesium Glycinate 300–400mg nightly to support methylation, insulin sensitivity, and inflammation reduction. Begin anti-inflammatory nutrition protocol: eliminate refined sugars and ultra-processed foods, increase oily fish (3× weekly), add turmeric with black pepper daily. Begin daily walking or low-intensity cardio (30 min/day) to support insulin sensitivity and lower hsCRP. Request GP referral for MTHFR genotype testing and repeat homocysteine in 6–8 weeks. Monitor fasting glucose daily if possible using a glucometer.",
    "group": "Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Methylated B-vitamins are the first-line BloomCell recommendation for elevated homocysteine with co-occurring low B12 and folate (BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx — Key Blood Results, homocysteine row). Magnesium supports both methylation cycle cofactor activity and glucose metabolism per BloomCell Nutrition MVP. Anti-inflammatory dietary protocol is triggered by hsCRP >1.0 mg/L per BloomCell priority action logic. Exercise is a Tier 1 Recovery strategy for insulin resistance pattern per BloomCell Recovery MVP. MTHFR testing triggered by homocysteine >10 umol/L per Key Supportive Testing sheet.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Rows 31, 14, 6, 22 | BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Rows 34, 41 | BloomCell Recovery MVP v2.xlsx - Recovery Strategies sheet - Column 15 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key supportive Testing sheet - Row 9",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "d8b9c0d1-e2f3-4345-a6bc-d7e8f9a0b1c2",
    "date": "06/25/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "TSH 0.5-2.0 uIU/mL 4.7 H | Free T3 3.0-4.0 pg/mL 2.6 L | Free T4 1.0-1.5 ng/dL 0.82 L | Ferritin 50-150 ng/mL 12 L | Hemoglobin 13.5-17.5 g/dL 11.8 L | Vitamin D, 25-OH Total 30-80 ng/mL 18.4 L",
    "group": "Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "The combination of elevated TSH (4.7 uIU/mL) with suppressed Free T3 (2.6 pg/mL) and Free T4 (0.82 ng/dL) represents a clinically significant sub-clinical hypothyroid pattern requiring sustained intervention over 3–6 months. This thyroid dysfunction is compounded by critically low Ferritin (12 ng/mL) — iron is a necessary cofactor for thyroid peroxidase (TPO) enzyme activity — and severe Vitamin D deficiency (18.4 ng/mL), which independently suppresses thyroid receptor sensitivity. This cluster represents a chronic, multi-system dysregulation requiring a long-term strategic approach rather than a single supplement or intervention.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Rows: TSH (Row 52), Free T3 (Row 42), Free T4 (Row 43), Ferritin (Row 28), Hemoglobin (Row 30), Vitamin D (Row 55) - Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  },
  {
    "id": "e9c0d1e2-f3a4-4456-b7cd-e8f9a0b1c2d3",
    "date": "06/25/26 10:15 AM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Target TSH <2.0 uIU/mL, Free T3 >3.0 pg/mL, Free T4 >1.0 ng/dL, Ferritin >70 ng/mL, Vitamin D >50 ng/mL, HbA1c <5.6%, Homocysteine <7 umol/L, hsCRP <1.0 mg/L over the next 3–6 months. Retest schedule: 6–8 weeks (TSH, Free T3, Free T4, Ferritin, hsCRP, homocysteine); 3 months (full metabolic panel: HbA1c, fasting glucose, lipid panel, Vitamin D, B12); 6 months (complete blood count, iron panel, thyroid antibodies TPO/thyroglobulin, full liver function). Long-term nutrition strategy: adopt a Mediterranean-style anti-inflammatory dietary pattern, prioritise iron-rich foods with Vitamin C co-ingestion, and limit glycaemic load. Consider Vitamin D3 + K2 supplementation (4000–5000 IU D3 daily with K2 100mcg) titrating to >50 ng/mL. Iron bisglycinate 25mg with Vitamin C 500mg on empty stomach. Thyroid-supportive nutrients: selenium 200mcg, zinc 15mg. DUTCH Plus adrenal/cortisol panel recommended to identify HPA-axis contribution to hypothyroid pattern. GI-MAP stool analysis recommended if Vitamin D and B12 fail to rise despite supplementation (malabsorption pathway).",
    "group": "Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Target values are drawn directly from BloomCell functional ranges in Biomarkers.xlsx. Retest schedule follows BloomCell monitoring cadence as structured in Master Report April 2026 (2).docx. Vitamin D3 + K2 protocol and iron bisglycinate recommendations sourced from BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx. Selenium and zinc for thyroid support from BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx Key Blood Results sheet (TSH/Free T3 rows). DUTCH Plus triggered by TSH elevation + possible adrenal/cortisol contribution per Key Supportive Testing sheet. GI-MAP triggered by multi-nutrient deficiency pattern (Vitamin D, B12, Ferritin all low simultaneously) per BloomCell malabsorption cluster logic.",
    "referencesource": "Master Report April 2026 (2).docx - Page 5 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key supportive Testing sheet - Rows 11, 18 | BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Rows 12, 34, 55 | Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Rows 42, 43, 52, 28, 30, 55",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  }
]
```
